steps.title: Use a simple scale
steps.content: Because all the listed $x$-values contain the same constant $s$, set $s=1$ to see the linear pattern with representative points $(-2,24)$, $(-1,21)$, and $(1,15)$.

steps.title: Find the representative line
steps.content:
The slope from $(-2,24)$ to $(-1,21)$ is:
$$\frac{21-24}{-1-(-2)}=-3$$
Using $(-1,21)$ in $y=-3x+b$ gives $b=18$, so the representative line is $y=-3x+18$.

steps.title: Restore the scale
steps.content:
Restoring the $s$ scale gives slope $-\frac{3}{s}$.
$$y=-\frac{3}{s}x+18$$
$$sy=-3x+18s$$
$$3x+sy=18s$$
The answer is B.
